dataset.full_name: A comprehensive dataset of magnetic resonance enterography images with bowel segment annotations
dataset.description: Whole gastrointestinal tract segmentation from magnetic resonance enterography images
dataset.target_structure: stomach, duodenum, small intestine, appendix, cecum, ascending colon, transverse colon, descending colon, sigmoid colon, rectum
dataset.acquisition_info: 3.0T MRI scanners (Siemens Prisma or Vida) with T2-weighted HASTE sequences in coronal orientation, slice thickness 4mm, voxel size 1.2×1.2×4.0mm or 1.4×1.4×4.0mm, patients received 1600-2000mL of 2.5% mannitol for bowel distension
dataset.annotation_info: Initial annotations by two experienced radiologists (3 and 6 years experience) using ITK-SNAP, followed by review and consensus by an abdominal imaging expert (>10 years experience), with iterative refinement using deep learning predictions
dataset.data_source: Single institution (First Affiliated Hospital of Sun Yat-sen University) retrospective clinical data
dataset.detailed_description: This dataset comprises magnetic resonance enterography (MRE) images from 114 patients with inflammatory bowel disease, focusing on whole gastrointestinal tract segmentation. The T2-weighted HASTE sequences were acquired on 3.0T Siemens scanners with coronal orientation and 4mm slice thickness. Each case includes pixel-level annotations of ten gastrointestinal segments: stomach, duodenum, small intestine, appendix, cecum, ascending colon, transverse colon, descending colon, sigmoid colon, and rectum. The annotations were created through a rigorous process involving multiple radiologists with iterative refinement using deep learning assistance.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate segmentation of gastrointestinal segments is essential for inflammatory bowel disease management, enabling precise localization of lesions, monitoring disease progression, and assessing treatment response in different bowel regions.
